response.extracted_text: The Human Cost: Waste, Administrative Friction, and Burnout Waste Category Administrative Complexity 265 Pricing Failure 230 Care Delivery Failure 165 Overtreatment 100 Fraud & Abuse 85 0 50 100 150 200 250 Estimated Waste ($B) A Systemic—Not Personal—Crisis Over $760 billion in annual healthcare waste is attributable to administrative complexity and care delivery failures—not patient volume or clinician stamina. Burnout is the downstream signal of a broken system. Physicians spend nearly 2 hours on administrative tasks for every 1 hour of direct patient care, eroding purpose, efficiency, and retention. Framing burnout as a resilience problem misses the structural root cause—and forfeits the opportunity to fix it.
response.layout_description: A large navy title spans the upper left/top of the slide. The lower left contains a horizontal bar chart, while the right side contains a bold heading, explanatory paragraphs, and a pink warning callout near the bottom.
response.slide_title: The Human Cost: Waste, Administrative Friction, and Burnout

Over $760 billion in annual healthcare waste is attributable to administrative complexity and care delivery failures—not patient volume or clinician stamina.
Burnout is the downstream signal of a broken system. Physicians spend nearly 2 hours on administrative tasks for every 1 hour of direct patient care, eroding purpose, efficiency, and retention.
Framing burnout as a resilience problem misses the structural root cause—and forfeits the opportunity to fix it.
